what_it_does: Registers an MCP tool named cc_clone that exposes seven operations for point-in-time cloning of Claude Code sessions, messaging clones, onboarding them, listing live clones, tearing them down, preparing operator-launch commands, and resolving clone:// addresses. It validates arguments locally and delegates all work to the runtime.cc_clone module.
observations: The file is a thin MCP-facing wrapper around runtime.cc_clone.
It defines an OPS tuple with seven literal operation names.
The register function uses @mcp.tool() to expose the inner cc_clone function.
fallback_model is split on commas into a list before being passed to the runtime.
provider accepts empty string, anthropic, or ollama and is forwarded to the runtime.
onboard supports fleet=true for parallel fleet-wide onboarding and a phase parameter.
resolve accepts a clone://<source_sid>/<at> URI and returns the clone record plus persisted reflection.
CloneError is caught and returned as an ok:false dict, while missing-argument checks raise ValueError.
The runtime module is imported inside the tool function, not at module load.
standouts: Point-in-time session reconstruction via compact:N, uuid:<id>, or ts:<iso> anchors.
Explicit split between safe supervised headless clones and operator-interactive channel clones.
Era-model substitution via model and comma-separated fallback_model, including an Ollama provider path.
clone:// URI addressing for clone lookup and persisted reflection.
conventions: Follows the MCP tool registration pattern with a register(mcp, suite) entry point.
Uses typing.Literal for the op parameter.
Heavy docstrings describe both file-level behavior and per-operation semantics.
Delegates implementation to a sibling runtime module rather than implementing logic locally.
Returns structured dicts from the tool instead of raw exceptions for runtime errors.
concerns: Missing-argument validation raises ValueError instead of returning the same ok:false shape used for CloneError, creating inconsistent error surfaces.
fallback_model string splitting is done in the tool layer, which could be surprising if the runtime expects a different format.
The suite parameter in register is unused, which may indicate an incomplete registration interface.
Lazy import of runtime inside the function hides dependency relationships from static analysis.
notes: This file is the agent-facing MCP surface for resurrecting and talking to past Claude Code sessions; the real clone lifecycle logic lives in runtime.cc_clone.
questions: What exact schema does runtime.cc_clone expect for source .jsonl transcripts and at anchors?
How does the supervisor-inject mechanism work, and what makes it the safe autonomous path?
What is the safety classifier that blocks agents from auto-launching interactive channel clones?
How are clone handles and session_ids mapped, and how does auto-pruning of closed clones work?
What does each onboard phase trigger in the runtime, and what is a persisted reflection?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it exposes the documented seven operations and forwards them to runtime.cc_clone after local argument checks.
intention_signals: To give agents a controlled, policy-aware way to resurrect historical Claude Code contexts for interrogation or onboarding while keeping unsupervised interactive channel launches out of reach.
novelty: URI-addressable historical clones, reflect-before-brief onboarding phases, explicit operator-launch preparation path, and era-model fallback substitution including local Ollama routing.
contribution: A single MCP tool surface that maps high-level clone operations to runtime implementations and encodes safety and policy distinctions in its parameter schema and docstrings.
summary_for_embedding: mcp_face/tools/cc_clone.py registers an MCP cc_clone tool that delegates point-in-time session cloning, messaging, fleet onboarding, listing, teardown, operator-launch preparation, and clone-address resolution to runtime.cc_clone.
intention_for_embedding: To provide agents with a safe, supervised interface for resurrecting and interrogating past Claude Code session contexts without directly launching interactive channel clones.

symbols.does: Registers the cc_clone MCP tool with the provided mcp instance.
symbols.description: The register function is the entry point for MCP tool auto-registration; it decorates the inner cc_clone function with @mcp.tool() and ignores the suite argument.

symbols.does: Handles all cc_clone operations by validating inputs and delegating to runtime.cc_clone.
symbols.description: This nested function implements the MCP tool surface for clone, msg, onboard, list, end, prepare, and resolve operations; it performs file-local argument checks, formats fallback_model as a comma-split list, and returns structured results or error dictionaries derived from runtime.cc_clone exceptions.
